mapping.a_element: quantum phase transition in a spin-1 Bose-Einstein condensate
mapping.b_element: isotropic to nematic phase transition in a uniaxial nematic liquid crystal
probability of string (disclination line) formation per bubble
theoretical value of string formation probability (1/π)

mapping.a_element: slow passage through a quantum critical point
mapping.b_element: Kibble mechanism of domain (bubble) formation during phase transition

brief_justification: Both papers study phase transitions with universal scaling or defect formation properties. The quantum phase transition's magnetization scaling corresponds to the Kibble mechanism's defect probability, and both rely on symmetry-breaking and topological defect formation during the transition, governed by analogous underlying principles (scaling laws and topological constraints).
